steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply the Exterior Angle Theorem
steps.content:
When a side of a triangle is extended, the exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles. Side BC is extended through C to D, making angle ACD an exterior angle.
$$\angle ACD = \angle A + \angle B$$
$$(x+y) = (4x+8) + (5x+4)$$
$$x + y = 9x + 12$$
$$y = 8x + 12$$

steps.title: Set Up the Triangle Angle Sum
steps.content:
The three interior angles of triangle ABC must sum to 180 degrees.
$$(4x+8) + (5x+4) + (5y+10) = 180$$
$$9x + 22 + 5y = 180$$
$$9x + 5y = 158$$

steps.title: Solve the System of Equations
steps.content:
Substitute $y = 8x + 12$ from the first equation into $9x + 5y = 158$:
$$9x + 5(8x + 12) = 158$$
$$9x + 40x + 60 = 158$$
$$49x = 98$$
$$x = 2$$
Then $y = 8(2) + 12 = 28$.

Therefore $x + y = 2 + 28 = 30$.
